body: DECLARE c CURSOR FOR SELECT 1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c CURSOR FOR SELECT 1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE c SCROLL CURSOR FOR SELECT 1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c SCROLL CURSOR FOR SELECT 1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE c NO SCROLL CURSOR FOR SELECT 1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c NO SCROLL CURSOR FOR SELECT 1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE c CURSOR IS SELECT 1; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c CURSOR FOR SELECT 1;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE c CURSOR (a int, b text) FOR SELECT a, b; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c CURSOR (a int, b text) FOR SELECT a, b;
BEGIN
END

comment: cursor with a typed arg using parens
body: DECLARE c CURSOR (n numeric(10,2)) FOR SELECT n; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
c CURSOR (n numeric(10,2)) FOR SELECT n;
BEGIN
END

body: DECLARE x ALIAS FOR y; BEGIN END
deparse:
DECLARE
x ALIAS FOR y;
BEGIN
END

comment: cursor and ordinary variables in one DECLARE section
body: DECLARE i int; c CURSOR FOR SELECT i; BEGIN END

comment: cursor query used by OPEN/FETCH in the body
body: DECLARE c CURSOR FOR SELECT 1; r int; BEGIN OPEN c; FETCH c INTO r; CLOSE c; END

comment: COLLATE in a cursor arg type is rejected, like PG (no decl_collate there)
body: DECLARE c CURSOR (a text COLLATE "C") FOR SELECT a; BEGIN END
